steps.args: {'prompt': "This is a free writing prospectus (pricing term sheet) for KKR & Co. Inc.'s Series D Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock offering in March 2025. Extract ALL key terms in detail: offering size (dollar amount and number of shares), over-allotment option, price to public, liquidation preference per share, dividend rate and dividend payment dates (and whether cumulative, payment in cash/stock), mandatory conversion date and conversion rates (minimum and maximum conversion rates, threshold appreciation price, initial price), early conversion terms (optional conversion rate, fundamental change provisions), voting rights, listing/ticker, use of proceeds, underwriters, trade date and settlement/closing date, and net proceeds. Document: {{kkr_fwp}}"}
steps.result:
# KKR & Co. Inc. — 6.25% Series D Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock
## Pricing Term Sheet Summary (FWP dated March 4, 2025)

---

## 1. Issuer & Security
- **Issuer:** KKR & Co. Inc., a Delaware corporation
- **Security:** 6.25% Series D Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock, par value $0.01 per share
- **CUSIP / ISIN:** 48251W 500 / US48251W5004

## 2. Offering Size
- **Shares offered:** 45,000,000 shares (**upsized from the previously announced 30,000,000 shares**)
- **Gross offering size:** $2.25 billion (45,000,000 × $50.00)
- **Over-allotment option:** 6,750,000 additional shares (would bring total to 51,750,000 shares / ~$2.5875 billion gross)

## 3. Pricing
- **Public offering price:** $50.00 per share
- **Underwriting discount:** $1.125 per share
- **Liquidation preference:** $50.00 per share

## 4. Dividends
- **Rate:** 6.25% per annum on the $50.00 liquidation preference (i.e., $3.125 per share per year)
- **First dividend (June 1, 2025):** approximately **$0.7292** per share (short first period)
- **Each subsequent quarterly dividend:** **$0.78125** per share
- **Dividend payment dates:** March 1, June 1, September 1 and December 1 of each year, commencing June 1, 2025, to and including March 1, 2028
- **Record dates:** February 15, May 15, August 15 and November 15 immediately preceding each payment date
- **Cumulative / form of payment:** Not specified in this term sheet — governed by the Preliminary Prospectus Supplement. (Mandatory convertible preferred of this type is customarily cumulative, payable when declared in cash, shares of common stock, or a combination; consult the prospectus supplement for the definitive terms.)

## 5. Mandatory Conversion
- **Mandatory Conversion Date:** Second business day following the last trading day of the 20-consecutive-trading-day period beginning on (and including) the 21st scheduled trading day immediately preceding March 1, 2028; **expected to be March 1, 2028**
- **Initial Price:** ~**$120.7729** ($50.00 ÷ Maximum Conversion Rate)
- **Threshold Appreciation Price:** ~**$150.9662** (~25% appreciation over the Initial Price; $50.00 ÷ Minimum Conversion Rate)
- **Floor Price:** $42.2705 (~35% of the Initial Price), subject to adjustment
- **Conversion rates (per preferred share, subject to anti-dilution adjustments):**

| Applicable Market Value of KKR common stock | Conversion Rate |
|---|---|
| Greater than Threshold Appreciation Price ($150.9662) | **0.3312** shares (Minimum Conversion Rate) |
| ≤ Threshold Appreciation Price but ≥ Initial Price | Between 0.3312 and 0.4140 shares ($50.00 ÷ Applicable Market Value) |
| Less than Initial Price ($120.7729) | **0.4140** shares (Maximum Conversion Rate) |

## 6. Early Conversion
- **Optional conversion by holder:** Any time prior to March 1, 2028 (other than during a Fundamental Change Conversion Period), holders may convert in whole or in part at the **Minimum Conversion Rate of 0.3312** shares of common stock per preferred share
- **Fundamental Change conversion:** If a Fundamental Change occurs on or prior to March 1, 2028, holders may convert (in whole or in part, but not in increments of less than one share) at the **Fundamental Change Conversion Rate**, determined by a table based on the Fundamental Change Effective Date and Fundamental Change Stock Price:
  - Rates range from **0.3280 to 0.4140** shares depending on effective date (March 7, 2025 through March 1, 2028) and stock price ($20.00 to $300.00)
  - Straight-line interpolation applies between table values (365/366-day year basis)
  - Stock price **above $300.00** → Minimum Conversion Rate (0.3312)
  - Stock price **below $20.00** → Maximum Conversion Rate (0.4140)
  - Converting holders also receive a **Fundamental Change Dividend Make-Whole Amount** (discounted at **4.77% per annum**) plus any **Accumulated Dividend Amount**

## 7. Voting Rights
- **Not detailed in this pricing term sheet** — set forth in the Preliminary Prospectus Supplement dated March 4, 2025 (typically limited voting rights, including the right to elect directors upon specified dividend arrearages, per customary mandatory convertible preferred terms).

## 8. Listing
- **Preferred stock:** Issuer intends to apply to list on the **NYSE under symbol "KKR PR D"**; if approved, trading expected to begin within 30 days after first issuance
- **Common stock:** KKR / NYSE

## 9. Use of Proceeds & Net Proceeds
- **Estimated net proceeds:** approximately **$2.20 billion** (approximately **$2.53 billion** if the over-allotment option is exercised in full), after underwriting discounts and estimated offering expenses
- **Use:** (i) acquisition of additional equity interests in core private equity portfolio companies reported in KKR's **Strategic Holdings segment**, and (ii) other general corporate purposes

## 10. Dates
- **Trade Date:** March 5, 2025
- **Settlement/Closing Date:** March 7, 2025 (**T+2**; purchasers wishing to trade before the business day preceding settlement must specify an alternate settlement cycle)

## 11. Underwriters
**Joint Book-Running Managers (20):**
Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC; KKR Capital Markets LLC; Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC; UBS Securities LLC; Barclays Capital Inc.; BofA Securities, Inc.; CIBC World Markets Corp.; Citigroup Global Markets Inc.; Evercore Group L.L.C.; HSBC Securities (USA) Inc.; J.P. Morgan Securities LLC; Keefe, Bruyette & Woods, Inc.; Mizuho Securities USA LLC; Nomura Securities International, Inc.; WR Securities, LLC; RBC Capital Markets, LLC; Scotia Capital (USA) Inc.; SMBC Nikko Securities America, Inc.; Truist Securities, Inc.; Wells Fargo Securities LLC

**Co-Managers (9):**
BTIG, LLC; Loop Capital Markets LLC; Oppenheimer & Co. Inc.; Raymond James & Associates, Inc.; American Veterans Group, PBC; C.L. King & Associates, Inc.; Guzman & Company; Strong Capital Markets, LLC; Tigress Financial Partners LLC

---

**Regulatory basis:** Filed pursuant to Rule 433; supplements the Preliminary Prospectus Supplement dated March 4, 2025 and base prospectus dated May 8, 2024 (Registration No. 333-279233). This term sheet supersedes inconsistent information in those documents.

steps.args: {'prompt': "This is KKR & Co. Inc.'s 8-K filed March 7, 2025 regarding its Series D Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock. Extract details on: whether the offering closed and on what date, number of shares issued (including whether the over-allotment option was exercised), whether dividends are cumulative and payable in cash/common stock or combination, dividend rights details, voting rights of the preferred stock (e.g., right to elect directors upon dividend arrearages, class voting rights), ranking, and any other key terms of the certificate of designations. Document: {{kkr_8k}}"}
steps.result:
# KKR & Co. Inc. — 8-K (Filed March 7, 2025): 6.25% Series D Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock

## Offering Closing & Shares Issued

| Item | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Closing date** | March 7, 2025 (Certificate of Designations filed with Delaware Secretary of State and effective same day) |
| **Shares issued** | 51,750,000 shares of 6.25% Series D Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock |
| **Aggregate liquidation preference** | $2,587,500,000 ($50.00 per share) |
| **Over-allotment option** | **Exercised in full** — 6,750,000 shares ($337,500,000 aggregate liquidation preference) of the total were issued pursuant to the underwriters' over-allotment option |
| **Net proceeds** | Approximately $2.53 billion, after underwriting discounts and estimated offering expenses |
| **Underwriting agreement** | Dated March 4, 2025, with Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC and KKR Capital Markets LLC as representatives of the underwriters |

## Dividend Rights

- **Rate:** 6.25% per annum on the $50.00 per share liquidation preference
- **Cumulative:** Yes — dividends are payable **on a cumulative basis** when, as and if declared by the board of directors (or an authorized committee)
- **Form of payment:** May be paid in **cash or, subject to certain limitations, in shares of Common Stock, or any combination of cash and Common Stock**
- **Payment dates:** Quarterly on March 1, June 1, September 1, and December 1 of each year, to and including March 1, 2028, **commencing June 1, 2025**

### Dividend Stopper / Restrictions on Junior and Parity Stock
- So long as any Series D shares are outstanding (subject to certain exceptions), **no dividends may be declared or paid on Common Stock or other junior stock**, and no junior stock may be purchased, redeemed, or otherwise acquired for consideration by the Company or its subsidiaries, unless all accumulated and unpaid dividends for all preceding dividend periods have been declared and paid (in cash, Common Stock, or a combination), or sufficient cash/shares have been set aside.
- **Parity stock restriction:** If Series D dividends (i) have not been declared and paid in full on a dividend payment date, or (ii) were declared but funds/shares were not set aside on the record date, then no dividends may be paid on parity stock unless dividends are declared on the Series D shares such that amounts are allocated **pro rata** between Series D holders and parity stock holders.

## Mandatory Conversion Terms

- **Mandatory conversion date:** Expected to be **March 1, 2028** (unless converted earlier per the Certificate of Designations)
- **Conversion ratio:** Each share converts into between **0.3312 and 0.4140 shares of Common Stock**, subject to customary anti-dilution adjustments
- **Determination:** Based on the average VWAP of Common Stock over the 20 consecutive trading day period beginning on (and including) the 21st scheduled trading day immediately prior to March 1, 2028

## Liquidation Preference & Ranking

- **Liquidation preference:** $50.00 per share, **plus accumulated and unpaid dividends (whether or not declared)** to, but excluding, the date fixed for liquidation, winding-up, or dissolution
- **Ranking (as reflected in the liquidation provisions):**
  - **Junior to:** Debt and other liabilities owed to creditors, and any stock ranking senior to the Series D Preferred
  - **Senior to:** Common Stock and any other junior-ranking stock — Series D holders are paid before any payment or distribution to junior stock

## Voting Rights

The 8-K states that the Certificate of Designations establishes **the voting rights of the Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock**, but the filing's narrative does **not itemize the specific voting terms** (e.g., the right to elect additional directors upon dividend arrearages or class voting rights on amendments/senior stock issuances). Those details are contained in the **Certificate of Designations itself, filed as Exhibit 3.1** and incorporated by reference. The 8-K's description is expressly "qualified in its entirety by reference" to that exhibit, so the full voting provisions (typical of mandatory convertibles, such as director election rights after six quarters of arrearages and class votes on senior stock or adverse charter amendments) must be confirmed in Exhibit 3.1.

## Other Key Terms / Exhibits

- The Certificate of Designations also establishes designations, powers, preferences, restrictions on issuance of additional shares of the same series or other classes/series, and conversion terms and conditions
- Item 5.03 confirms the Certificate of Designations constitutes an amendment to the Company's charter documents
- **Exhibits:** Underwriting Agreement (1.1); Certificate of Designations (3.1); Form of Series D Stock Certificate (4.1, included in 3.1); Simpson Thacher & Bartlett LLP legality opinion (5.1)

steps.args: {'prompt': "This is the Certificate of Designations for KKR & Co. Inc.'s 6.25% Series D Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock. Describe in detail the VOTING RIGHTS section: do holders have general voting rights? What happens if dividends are in arrears (right to elect directors — how many quarters of arrears, how many directors)? What class voting rights exist (e.g., approval required for senior stock issuance, adverse charter amendments)? Also confirm the ranking provisions (parity with Series C preferred if any). Document: {{kkr_cod}}"}
steps.result:
# Voting Rights (Section 7) — 6.25% Series D Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock

## 1. General Voting Rights — None

Under **Section 7(a)**, holders of the Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock have **no general voting rights**. They have only the voting powers specifically set forth in Section 7, plus whatever is specifically required by Delaware law or by the Charter. They do not vote with the common stock on ordinary matters (director elections, etc.).

## 2. Right to Elect Directors Upon Dividend Arrears (Section 7(b))

- **Trigger ("Nonpayment"):** Dividends not declared and paid for the equivalent of **six or more Dividend Periods** (i.e., six quarters), **whether or not consecutive**.
- **Remedy:** The authorized size of the Board is **automatically increased by two**, and holders — **voting together as a single class with all other series of "Voting Preferred Stock"** (parity stock with like voting powers) — may elect **two additional directors ("Preferred Stock Directors")** at the next annual meeting or a special meeting.
- **Limits on the right:**
  - Election of Preferred Stock Directors may not cause the Corporation to violate NYSE (or other exchange) requirements that a majority of directors be independent.
  - The Board may **never include more than two** Preferred Stock Directors (regardless of how many preferred series are in arrears).
- **Special meeting mechanics:** Holders of at least **25%** of the Series D shares (together with holders of other Voting Preferred Stock) may **request a special meeting** to elect the Preferred Stock Directors — unless the next annual/special meeting is already scheduled within 90 days, in which case the election is added to that meeting's agenda.
- **Quorum/vote:** A majority in voting power of the outstanding Series D plus other Voting Preferred Stock constitutes a quorum; a majority of voting power present elects the directors. Voting power is measured **by liquidation preference** (Section 7(f)).
- **Termination ("Nonpayment Remedy"):** Once all accumulated and unpaid dividends are **paid in full** (or declared with sufficient cash/shares set aside), the voting power **immediately terminates** (subject to revesting on a future Nonpayment), the Preferred Stock Directors are **automatically disqualified**, their terms end, and the Board size automatically shrinks by two.
- **Removal/vacancies (Section 7(b)(iv)):** Preferred Stock Directors may be removed **with or without cause** by a majority in voting power of the outstanding Series D and other Voting Preferred Stock (single class). Vacancies may generally be filled by the remaining Preferred Stock Director's written consent, or by holder vote if the vacancy resulted from removal or no Preferred Stock Director remains.

## 3. Class Voting Rights (Section 7(c)) — Two-Thirds Vote Required

So long as any Series D shares are outstanding, the Corporation may not, without the affirmative vote or consent of **at least two-thirds in voting power** of the outstanding Series D shares (and, for clause (i) only, together with all other Voting Preferred Stock, voting as a single class):

1. **Senior Stock:** Amend or alter the Charter to **authorize, create, or increase** the authorized amount of any class or series of **Senior Stock**;
2. **Adverse amendments:** Amend, alter or repeal any provision of the Charter or the Certificate of Designations so as to **materially and adversely affect** the special rights, preferences or voting powers of the Series D; or
3. **Share exchange / reclassification / merger:** Consummate a binding share exchange, reclassification, merger or consolidation involving the Series D, **unless** (i) the Series D remains outstanding (or is converted into/exchanged for preference securities of the survivor or its ultimate parent) and (ii) the surviving securities' rights, preferences and voting powers, taken as a whole, are **not materially less favorable** than those of the Series D immediately before the transaction.

If a transaction triggers both clauses (ii) and (iii), **clause (iii) governs**.

**Carve-outs — deemed NOT adverse (no vote required):**
- Any increase in authorized but unissued Preferred Stock;
- Any increase in authorized or issued **Series D** shares;
- The creation/issuance (or increase) of any **Parity Stock or Junior Stock**;
- Application of the Reorganization Event provisions of Section 15.

Also, if a matter adversely affects some but not all series of Voting Preferred Stock, only the affected series vote as a class.

**Amendments without holder consent (Section 7(d)):** The Corporation may amend the terms without any vote to cure ambiguities/mistakes, make non-inconsistent provisions, make changes not materially adverse to holders, conform the terms to the Prospectus Supplement description, or file a certificate of correction.

## 4. Ranking (Section 2) — and the Series C Question

The Series D ranks, as to dividends and liquidation distributions:
- **Senior** to Junior Stock — expressly including the **Common Stock** and the **Existing Series I Preferred Stock** ($0.01 liquidation value), and any future stock not expressly ranked senior or on parity;
- **On parity** with Parity Stock — defined as classes/series **established after the Initial Issue Date (March 7, 2025)** whose terms expressly provide for parity ranking;
- **Junior** to any Senior Stock (future stock expressly ranked senior — itself requiring the 2/3 class vote above) and to all existing and future **indebtedness and other liabilities**; and
- **Structurally subordinated** to indebtedness and liabilities of the Corporation's subsidiaries.

**On Series C:** This Certificate of Designations contains **no reference to a Series C preferred stock** and does not rank the Series D on parity with any Series C. KKR's prior 6.00% Series C Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock had already mandatorily converted (in 2023) and was no longer outstanding at the March 2025 issue date, so the only pre-existing preferred addressed is the **Series I**, which is expressly classified as **Junior Stock**. Any "Parity Stock" under this instrument can only be a series created *after* March 7, 2025 with express parity terms.

steps.args:
{'final_result': 'SUMMARY OF KKR & CO. INC. 6.25% SERIES D MANDATORY CONVERTIBLE PREFERRED STOCK (March 2025 offering)

1) SIZE OF OFFERING
- Announced March 4, 2025 at ~$1.5 billion, then upsized and priced the same day at $2.25 billion (45,000,000 shares at $50.00 per share).
- Underwriters were granted a 30-day over-allotment option for up to an additional $337.5 million (6,750,000 shares), which was EXERCISED IN FULL. Total issuance at closing: 51,750,000 shares, aggregate liquidation preference of $2,587,500,000 (~$2.59 billion).
- Net proceeds: approximately $2.53 billion after underwriting discounts ($1.125/share) and estimated offering expenses (~$2.20 billion before the over-allotment exercise).
- Lead joint book-running managers: Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC and KKR Capital Markets LLC (as representatives), with 18 additional bookrunners and 9 co-managers.

2) CLOSING DATE
- Trade date: March 5, 2025; the offering closed (settled) on March 7, 2025 (T+2), the same day the Certificate of Designations became effective in Delaware.

3) PRICE
- Price to public: $50.00 per share (underwriting discount of $1.125 per share).

4) LIQUIDATION PREFERENCE
- $50.00 per share, plus accumulated and unpaid dividends (whether or not declared) to, but excluding, the liquidation date. Aggregate liquidation preference of $2.5875 billion. The Series D ranks senior to KKR common stock and the existing Series I preferred stock (junior stock), on parity with any future parity stock, and junior to any future senior stock and to all indebtedness/liabilities (and is structurally subordinated to subsidiary liabilities).

5) DIVIDEND RIGHTS
- Rate: 6.25% per annum on the $50.00 liquidation preference ($3.125 per share annually; $0.78125 per regular quarterly period).
- Cumulative, payable when, as and if declared by the board, quarterly on March 1, June 1, September 1 and December 1, commencing June 1, 2025 and ending March 1, 2028. First (short) dividend: ~$0.7292 per share.
- Payable in cash or, subject to limitations, in shares of common stock, or a combination.
- Dividend stopper: while Series D is outstanding, KKR generally may not pay dividends on or repurchase junior stock (including common stock) unless all accumulated Series D dividends are paid or set aside; parity stock dividends must be shared pro rata if Series D is in arrears.

6) CONVERSION TERMS
- Mandatory conversion: on the mandatory conversion date, expected March 1, 2028 (second business day following the 20-trading-day averaging period beginning on the 21st scheduled trading day before March 1, 2028), each share automatically converts into between 0.3312 shares (minimum conversion rate) and 0.4140 shares (maximum conversion rate) of KKR common stock, based on the average VWAP over that 20-day period, subject to customary anti-dilution adjustments.
  - Initial price: ~$120.7729 (=$50.00 / 0.4140) — if the market value is below this, holders receive the maximum rate of 0.4140.
  - Threshold appreciation price: ~$150.9662 (~25% above the initial price; =$50.00 / 0.3312) — if the market value exceeds this, holders receive the minimum rate of 0.3312; between the two prices, holders receive $50.00 of value ($50.00 / applicable market value).
  - Floor price: $42.2705 (~35% of the initial price), subject to adjustment.
- Optional early conversion: holders may convert at any time before March 1, 2028 at the minimum conversion rate of 0.3312 shares.
- Fundamental change: upon a fundamental change on or before March 1, 2028, holders may convert during the fundamental change conversion period at a fundamental change conversion rate (0.3280–0.4140 shares depending on effective date and stock price, per a make-whole table) and also receive a fundamental change dividend make-whole amount (future dividends discounted at 4.77% per annum) plus any accumulated dividend amount.

7) VOTING RIGHTS
- No general voting rights (holders do not vote with common stock on ordinary matters).
- Director election upon nonpayment: if dividends are in arrears for six or more dividend periods (whether or not consecutive), the board is increased by two and Series D holders (voting as a single class with other voting preferred stock, by liquidation preference) may elect two additional "Preferred Stock Directors"; this right terminates when all arrears are paid/set aside.
- Class voting rights: consent of at least two-thirds of the outstanding Series D is required to (i) authorize/create/increase senior stock, (ii) amend the charter/certificate of designations so as to materially and adversely affect Series D rights, or (iii) effect certain mergers, share exchanges or reclassifications unless the Series D survives (or is exchanged for securities) with rights not materially less favorable. Creation of parity or junior stock and increases in authorized Series D shares are expressly deemed not adverse and require no vote.

8) PURPOSE / USE OF PROCEEDS
- KKR intends to use the net proceeds (~$2.53 billion) to (i) acquire additional equity interests in core private equity portfolio companies reported in its Strategic Holdings segment and (ii) for other general corporate purposes. (Concurrent with the pricing, KKR announced plans to increase its ownership in three Strategic Holdings companies — USI Insurance Services, 1-800 Contacts and Heartland Dental.)

9) LISTING
- KKR applied to list the Series D on the NYSE under the symbol "KKR PR D," with trading expected to begin within 30 days of issuance.

            "name": "KKR Upsizes and Prices Offering of Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock (press release, March 4, 2025)"
        }
    ]
}'}
